name: Manhattan Ferry Landing, Cortlandt Street Slip
description: The north Manhattan shore in near-total darkness, where bateaux ground against the mud bank and men climb out into shallow water to clear the hull for the return crossing. A single hooded lantern marks the landing point.
atmosphericDetails: The sound of men wading through shallow water and climbing the bank in the dark, equipment clanking despite orders for silence.
The hooded lantern swinging gently on its post, the only navigational reference point for bateaux coming in through the fog.
The voices of Manhattan-side officers directing the landed troops away from the slip to clear space for the next wave.
The smell of the Manhattan shore—different from Brooklyn, with more wood smoke and less river mud, the smell of a city rather than a fortification.
